1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Project Overview
     4→
     5→Partio App is a Next.js 16 dashboard for browsing AI agent checkpoint data stored in GitHub repos. It uses the `src/` directory layout with the App Router.
     6→
     7→## Commands
     8→
     9→- `npm run dev` — start dev server
    10→- `npm run build` — production build
    11→- `npm run lint` — run ESLint
    12→- `npm start` — start production server
    13→
    14→## Architecture
    15→
    16→- **Framework**: Next.js 16 with App Router and `src/` directory
    17→- **Auth**: NextAuth v5 beta (`src/lib/auth.ts`) with GitHub OAuth. The `auth()` wrapper is used in middleware and API routes to access the session.
    18→- **Middleware**: `src/middleware.ts` — must live in `src/` (not the project root) because the app uses `src/app/`. Redirects unauthenticated users to `/login`.
    19→- **Data fetching**: SWR hooks in `src/hooks/` call internal API routes under `src/app/api/github/`. API routes use Octokit with the user's GitHub access token from the session.
    20→- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4 with dark theme. CSS variables defined in `src/app/globals.css`.
    21→- **Route groups**: `(auth)` for the login page, `(dashboard)` for the authenticated layout with sidebar/topbar.
    22→
    23→## Conventions
    24→
    25→- Path alias `@/*` maps to `src/*` (configured in `tsconfig.json`)
    26→- Client components use `"use client"` directive; server components are the default
    27→- API route handlers authenticate via `auth()` from `@/lib/auth` and return early with 401 if no session
    28→- UI components live in `src/components/ui/`, layout components in `src/components/layout/`
    29→- Types are in `src/types/`
    30→
    31→## Key Files
    32→
    33→- `src/lib/auth.ts` — NextAuth config (GitHub provider, JWT/session callbacks)
    34→- `src/middleware.ts` — auth redirect middleware
    35→- `src/app/api/github/repos/route.ts` — lists repos with checkpoint counts
    36→- `src/types/checkpoint.ts` — shared TypeScript interfaces
    37→- `src/hooks/use-repos.ts` / `src/hooks/use-checkpoints.ts` — SWR hooks
    38→
    39→## Common Pitfalls
    40→
    41→- Middleware must be at `src/middleware.ts`, not the project root — Next.js only picks it up at the same level as the `app/` directory.
    42→- NextAuth v5 is a beta (`5.0.0-beta.30`). The `auth()` function serves as both a session getter and a middleware wrapper.
    43→- Environment variables (`GITHUB_CLIENT_ID`, `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`, `AUTH_SECRET`) must be set in `.env.local`.
    44→

1→# Partio App
     2→
     3→Dashboard for viewing and exploring AI agent checkpoint data stored in GitHub repositories.
     4→
     5→## Features
     6→
     7→- **GitHub OAuth** sign-in via NextAuth
     8→- **Repository browser** with search and filtering (defaults to repos with checkpoints)
     9→- **Checkpoint list** per repo (read from the `partio/checkpoints/v1` branch)
    10→- **Checkpoint detail** with tabbed interface:
    11→  - **Sessions** tab — transcript viewer with collapsible sessions, tool usage pills, user avatars, and merged assistant messages
    12→  - **Plan** tab — rendered markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks, GFM table support, copy/download actions
    13→  - **Files** tab — commit diff viewer
    14→- **Checkpoint actions** — contextual action cards per tab (e.g. "Resume Claude Code Session" with CLI command)
    15→- **Overview dashboard** with stats and activity heatmap
    16→- **Deep-linking** via `?tab=` query parameters
    17→
    18→## Tech Stack
    19→
    20→- [Next.js](https://nextjs.org/) 16 (App Router, `src/` directory)
    21→- [React](https://react.dev/) 19
    22→- [NextAuth](https://authjs.dev/) v5 (beta) with GitHub provider
    23→- [Octokit](https://github.com/octokit/rest.js) for GitHub API access
    24→- [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) v4
    25→- [SWR](https://swr.vercel.app/) for data fetching
    26→- [Framer Motion](https://www.framer.com/motion/) for animations
    27→- [react-markdown](https://github.com/remarkjs/react-markdown) + [remark-gfm](https://github.com/remarkjs/remark-gfm) for markdown rendering
    28→- [react-syntax-highlighter](https://github.com/react-syntax-highlighter/react-syntax-highlighter) for code block highlighting
    29→- TypeScript
    30→
    31→## Getting Started
    32→
    33→### Prerequisites
    34→
    35→- Node.js 20+
    36→- A GitHub OAuth App (create one at https://github.com/settings/developers)
    37→
    38→### Environment Variables
    39→
    40→Create a `.env.local` file:
    41→
    42→```
    43→GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=<your-github-client-id>
    44→GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=<your-github-client-secret>
    45→AUTH_SECRET=<random-secret-for-nextauth>
    46→```
    47→
    48→Generate `AUTH_SECRET` with:
    49→
    50→```sh
    51→openssl rand -base64 32
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Install and Run
    55→
    56→```sh
    57→npm install
    58→npm run dev
    59→```
    60→
    61→Open http://localhost:3000. You will be redirected to the GitHub login page.
    62→
    63→### Build
    64→
    65→```sh
    66→npm run build
    67→npm start
    68→```
    69→
    70→## Project Structure
    71→
    72→```
    73→src/
    74→  app/
    75→    (auth)/login/         Login page
    76→    (dashboard)/          Dashboard layout (sidebar + topbar)
    77→      page.tsx            Overview with stats and heatmap
    78→      repositories/       Repository list with search/filter
    79→      [owner]/[repo]/     Checkpoint list for a repo
    80→        [checkpointId]/   Checkpoint detail (transcript + diff)
    81→    api/
    82→      auth/               NextAuth route handler
    83→      github/             GitHub API proxy routes
    84→        repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[id]/
    85→          diff/           Checkpoint diff API
    86→          plan/           Checkpoint plan API
    87→          session/        Checkpoint session API
    88→  components/
    89→    layout/               Sidebar, topbar, nav items
    90→    providers/            Session provider
    91→    ui/                   Reusable UI components
    92→      checkpoint-actions.tsx  Contextual action cards per tab
    93→      plan-viewer.tsx     Markdown plan renderer
    94→      tool-badge.tsx      Tool usage pill component
    95→  hooks/                  SWR data-fetching hooks
    96→  lib/
    97→    auth.ts               NextAuth configuration
    98→    github.ts             Octokit factory
    99→    github/               GitHub API helpers (repos, checkpoints, diff, session, plan)
   100→      plan.ts             Plan markdown fetching/parsing
   101→    utils.ts              Utility functions
   102→  middleware.ts           Auth middleware (redirects unauthenticated users to /login)
   103→  types/                  TypeScript type definitions
   104→```
   105→

input.prompt:
Analyze the HAR file at /Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har for performance bottlenecks. This is for the app at app.partio.io.

DO NOT edit any files. This is a read-only research task.

Use bash to parse the HAR file (it's JSON). Extract and analyze:

1. **Total page load waterfall**: How many requests, total transfer size, total time
2. **Slowest requests**: Top 10 slowest requests by response time (URL, method, time, size)
3. **Request chain/waterfall**: Are there sequential API calls that could be parallelized?
4. **Large responses**: Any oversized API responses or assets
5. **API endpoints called**: List all API/XHR requests with their timing
6. **Asset loading**: JS bundle sizes, CSS, images, fonts
7. **DNS/TLS/Connection overhead**: Any repeated connection setup
8. **Caching headers**: Are responses properly cached? Check Cache-Control headers
9. **Compression**: Are responses gzip/brotli compressed?
10. **Blocking resources**: Any render-blocking resources

Use jq or python to parse the HAR JSON. The HAR format has entries under .log.entries. Each entry has:
- .request.url, .request.method
- .response.status, .response.content.size, .response.content.mimeType
- .response.headers (array of {name, value})
- .time (total time in ms)
- .timings (dns, connect, ssl, send, wait, receive)

Provide a comprehensive performance analysis with specific numbers and recommendations.

input.prompt:
Analyze the HAR file at /Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har for performance patterns. This is for entire.io's "overview" section - a competitor/reference app.

DO NOT edit any files. This is a read-only research task.

Use bash to parse the HAR file (it's JSON). Extract and analyze:

1. **Total page load waterfall**: How many requests, total transfer size, total time
2. **Slowest requests**: Top 10 slowest requests by response time (URL, method, time, size)
3. **Request chain/waterfall**: Are there sequential API calls that could be parallelized?
4. **Large responses**: Any oversized API responses or assets
5. **API endpoints called**: List all API/XHR requests with their timing
6. **Asset loading**: JS bundle sizes, CSS, images, fonts
7. **DNS/TLS/Connection overhead**: Any repeated connection setup
8. **Caching headers**: Are responses properly cached? Check Cache-Control headers
9. **Compression**: Are responses gzip/brotli compressed?
10. **Render-blocking resources**: Any blocking resources
11. **Features detected**: What data/features does the overview page load? (contributions graph, stats, repositories, activity feed, etc.)

Use jq or python to parse the HAR JSON. The HAR format has entries under .log.entries. Each entry has:
- .request.url, .request.method
- .response.status, .response.content.size, .response.content.mimeType
- .response.headers (array of {name, value})
- .time (total time in ms)
- .timings (dns, connect, ssl, send, wait, receive)

Provide a comprehensive performance analysis with specific numbers. Also identify what features/data the overview loads so we can compare with partio.

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
from datetime import datetime

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("1. TOTAL PAGE LOAD WATERFALL SUMMARY")
print("=" * 80)

total_requests = len(entries)
total_transfer = sum(e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0) for e in entries)
total_time = sum(e.get('time', 0) for e in entries)

# Calculate wall-clock time from first to last request
start_times = []
end_times = []
for e in entries:
    st = e.get('startedDateTime', '')
    t = e.get('time', 0)
    if st:
        start_times.append(st)
        # We'll compute end based on start + time
        
# Sort by start time
sorted_entries = sorted(entries, key=lambda x: x.get('startedDateTime', ''))
first_start = sorted_entries[0]['startedDateTime']
last_entry = max(entries, key=lambda e: datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() + e.get('time', 0)/1000)
last_end_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(last_entry['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() + last_entry.get('time', 0)/1000
first_start_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(sorted_entries[0]['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp()
wall_clock = (last_end_ts - first_start_ts) * 1000

# Sizes
total_content_size = sum(e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0) for e in entries)
total_header_size = sum(e.get('response', {}).get('headersSize', 0) for e in entries)
total_body_size = sum(e.get('response', {}).get('bodySize', 0) for e in entries if e.get('response', {}).get('bodySize', 0) > 0)

print(f"Total Requests:           {total_requests}")
print(f"Total Content Size:       {total_content_size:,} bytes ({total_content_size/1024:.1f} KB)")
print(f"Total Body (transferred): {total_body_size:,} bytes ({total_body_size/1024:.1f} KB)")
print(f"Total Header Size:        {total_header_size:,} bytes")
print(f"Cumulative Request Time:  {total_time:,.1f} ms")
print(f"Wall-Clock Time:          {wall_clock:,.1f} ms ({wall_clock/1000:.2f} s)")

# By resource type
print("\nBy Resource Type:")
types = {}
for e in entries:
    rt = e.get('_resourceType', 'unknown')
    if rt not in types:
        types[rt] = {'count': 0, 'size': 0, 'time': 0}
    types[rt]['count'] += 1
    types[rt]['size'] += e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    types[rt]['time'] += e.get('time', 0)

for rt, v in sorted(types.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]['size']):
    print(f"  {rt:15s}: {v['count']:3d} requests, {v['size']/1024:8.1f} KB, {v['time']:8.1f} ms total time")

python3 -c "
import json
with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)
entries = har['log']['entries']

# 3. Request chain/waterfall - show all requests in order with start times
print('=== 3. REQUEST WATERFALL (chronological) ===')
# Parse startedDateTime to get relative timing
from datetime import datetime

first_start = None
for i, e in enumerate(entries):
    start_str = e.get('startedDateTime', '')
    url = e['request']['url']
    method = e['request']['method']
    time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
    
    # Parse ISO datetime
    try:
        if 'Z' in start_str:
            start_str = start_str.replace('Z', '+00:00')
        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(start_str)
        if first_start is None:
            first_start = dt
        relative_ms = (dt - first_start).total_seconds() * 1000
    except:
        relative_ms = -1
    
    # Shorten URL
    if len(url) > 80:
        url_short = url[:80] + '...'
    else:
        url_short = url
    
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    print(f'{i+1:2d}. [{relative_ms:7.0f}ms -> {relative_ms+time_ms:7.0f}ms] ({time_ms:6.0f}ms) {method:4s} {url_short} [{size/1024:.1f}KB]')
" 2>&1

python3 -c "
import json
with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)
entries = har['log']['entries']

# 5. API/XHR endpoints
print('=== 5. API ENDPOINTS ===')
for e in entries:
    url = e['request']['url']
    method = e['request']['method']
    mime = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('mimeType', 'unknown')
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
    status = e['response']['status']
    
    # Identify API calls - look for JSON responses, fetch/XHR, or API paths
    is_api = False
    if 'json' in mime.lower():
        is_api = True
    if '/api/' in url or '/_next/data/' in url:
        is_api = True
    # Check for x-requested-with or similar headers
    for h in e['request'].get('headers', []):
        if h['name'].lower() == 'x-requested-with':
            is_api = True
    # Check initiator type
    initiator = e.get('_initiator', {}).get('type', '')
    resource_type = e.get('_resourceType', '')
    if resource_type in ('xhr', 'fetch'):
        is_api = True
    
    if is_api:
        if len(url) > 100:
            url = url[:100] + '...'
        print(f'{method:6s} {status} {time_ms:7.1f}ms {size:>10,} bytes  {url}')
        print(f'       MIME: {mime} | Resource: {resource_type}')
        # Show response snippet if JSON
        content_text = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('text', '')
        if content_text and 'json' in mime.lower():
            try:
                data = json.loads(content_text)
                if isinstance(data, dict):
                    print(f'       Keys: {list(data.keys())[:15]}')
                elif isinstance(data, list):
                    print(f'       Array of {len(data)} items')
                    if data:
                        print(f'       First item keys: {list(data[0].keys())[:15] if isinstance(data[0], dict) else type(data[0]).__name__}')
            except:
                pass
        print()

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
from datetime import datetime

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("3. REQUEST CHAIN / WATERFALL ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)

# Sort by start time
sorted_entries = sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('startedDateTime', ''))
base_time = datetime.fromisoformat(sorted_entries[0]['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000

print("\nFull waterfall (offset from first request):")
print(f"{'#':>3} {'Start':>8} {'Duration':>8} {'Type':>10} {'Status':>6} {'Size':>8} URL")
print("-" * 120)

for i, e in enumerate(sorted_entries):
    ts = datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000
    offset = ts - base_time
    time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
    url = e['request']['url']
    status = e.get('response', {}).get('status', 0)
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    
    # Shorten URL
    if 'partio.io' in url:
        url = url.replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 65:
        url = url[:62] + '...'
    
    bar_start = int(offset / 100)
    bar_len = max(1, int(time_ms / 100))
    
    print(f"{i+1:3d} {offset:7.0f}ms {time_ms:7.0f}ms {rtype:>10} {status:>6} {size/1024:7.1f}KB {url}")

print("\n\nSEQUENTIAL API CALLS (fetch type):")
print("-" * 80)
fetch_entries = [(i, e) for i, e in enumerate(sorted_entries) if e.get('_resourceType') == 'fetch']
for idx, (i, e) in enumerate(fetch_entries):
    ts = datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000
    offset = ts - base_time
    time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
    end = offset + time_ms
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 70:
        url = url[:67] + '...'
    print(f"  [{offset:7.0f}ms - {end:7.0f}ms] ({time_ms:6.0f}ms) {url}")

# Detect sequential patterns
print("\nSequential dependency analysis:")
for idx in range(len(fetch_entries) - 1):
    _, e1 = fetch_entries[idx]
    _, e2 = fetch_entries[idx + 1]
    end1 = datetime.fromisoformat(e1['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000 - base_time + e1.get('time', 0)
    start2 = datetime.fromisoformat(e2['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000 - base_time
    gap = start2 - end1
    if gap >= -50 and gap <= 100:  # Started within 100ms of previous ending
        url1 = e1['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '').split('?')[0]
        url2 = e2['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '').split('?')[0]
        print(f"  SEQUENTIAL: {url1[:50]} -> {url2[:50]} (gap: {gap:.0f}ms)")

PYEOF

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the Next.js app. Let me provide a detailed report:

## Comprehensive Next.js App Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Partio Next.js dashboard application. Here's a complete breakdown of the project:

### 1. Project Context (from CLAUDE.md)
**Partio** is a Next.js 16 dashboard for browsing AI agent checkpoint data stored in GitHub repositories. It uses the `src/` directory layout with the App Router and requires GitHub OAuth authentication.

**Key Tech Stack:**
- Next.js 16 with App Router
- React 19
- NextAuth v5 (beta) with GitHub OAuth
- SWR for client-side data fetching
- Tailwind CSS v4 with dark theme
- Octokit for GitHub API access
- Framer Motion, react-markdown, react-syntax-highlighter

---

### 2. Full Project Structure

**Root Layout:**
```
src/
  app/                           # Next.js App Router
    (auth)/login/               # Login page (public route group)
    (dashboard)/                # Main dashboard (protected route group)
      page.tsx                  # Overview dashboard with stats & heatmap
      [owner]/[repo]/           # Repository detail pages
        checkpoints/            # Checkpoint list page
          [checkpointId]/       # Checkpoint detail page (tabbed interface)
        code/                   # Code browsing
        commits/                # Commit list & detail
        issues/                 # Issues management
        pulls/                  # Pull requests
      repositories/             # Repository browser with search
      settings/                 # Settings page
      layout.tsx                # Dashboard layout (sidebar + topbar)
    api/
      auth/[...nextauth]/       # NextAuth route handler
      github/repos/             # GitHub API proxy routes
        route.ts                # List repos with checkpoint counts
        recent-commits/         # Recent checkpoints for overview
        [owner]/[repo]/
          checkpoints/          # List checkpoints for repo
            [id]/               # Individual checkpoint data
              diff/route.ts     # Checkpoint diff API
              session/route.ts  # Checkpoint transcript API
              plan/route.ts     # Checkpoint plan API
          commits/              # Commit APIs
          issues/               # Issue APIs
          pulls/                # PR APIs
      themes/                   # Theme customization APIs
    layout.tsx                  # Root layout with providers
    globals.css                 # Global styles & CSS variables
  
  components/
    layout/
      sidebar.tsx               # Main sidebar navigation
      topbar.tsx                # Top header with user info
      nav-item.tsx              # Navigation item component
    providers/
      session-provider.tsx      # NextAuth SessionProvider wrapper
      theme-provider.tsx        # Theme context provider
    ui/                         # Reusable UI components
      overview-stats.tsx        # Stats cards for overview page
      heatmap.tsx               # Activity heatmap (91 days)
      stat-card.tsx             # Individual stat card

  hooks/                        # SWR data-fetching hooks
    use-repos.ts                # List repos with checkpoints
    use-checkpoints.ts          # List/get checkpoints, session, diff, plan
    use-recent-commits.ts       # Recent checkpoints for overview
    use-commits.ts              # List/get commits
    use-issues.ts               # List/get issues

    use-activity.ts             # Activity data
    use-branches.ts             # Branch data

  lib/

```

---

### 3. Data Fetching Architecture

**Pattern: SWR (Stale-While-Revalidate) with Client Components**

All data fetching is done through SWR hooks that call internal Next.js API routes:

```typescript
// Example: use-repos.ts
export function useRepos() {
  const { data, error, isLoading } = useSWR<RepoWithCheckpoints[]>(
    "/api/github/repos",
    fetcher  // simple fetch wrapper
  );
  return { repos: data, isLoading, isError: !!error };
}
```

**Data Fetching Flow:**
1. Client component calls SWR hook
2. Hook makes fetch request to internal API route (`/api/github/repos`)
3. API route checks auth via `auth()` from NextAuth
4. Route uses Octokit with user's GitHub access token from session
5. Route makes GitHub API calls and returns data as JSON
6. SWR handles caching, revalidation, and error states

**Key Features:**
- **Deduplication**: Multiple requests for same URL within 30s are deduplicated
- **Revalidation**: `revalidateOnFocus: false` prevents refetch when window regains focus
- **Error handling**: Errors throw from fetcher, caught by SWR's error state
- **Loading states**: `isLoading` flag for conditional rendering

---

### 4. API Endpoints Overview

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `/api/github/repos` | List authenticated user's repos with checkpoint counts |
| `/api/github/repos/recent-commits` | Recent checkpoints for overview (heatmap, stats) |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]` | Repo detail |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints` | List checkpoints for a repo |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[id]` | Get single checkpoint metadata |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[id]/diff` | Get checkpoint diff (unified diff format) |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[id]/session` | Get checkpoint transcript (JSONL → JSON array) |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[id]/plan` | Get checkpoint markdown plan |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/commits` | List commits |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/commits/[sha]` | Get commit detail |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/issues` | List issues |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/issues/[number]` | Get issue detail |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/pulls` | List pull requests |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/pulls/[number]` | Get PR detail |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/contents` | File tree & content |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/contributors` | Repo contributors |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/branches` | List branches |
| `/api/github/repos/[owner]/[repo]/activity` | Activity data |

---

### 5. Caching Strategies

**1. SWR Client-Side Caching:**
- Default deduplication interval: 30 seconds
- Configurable via `dedupingInterval` option
- Automatic cache revalidation on window focus (configurable)

**2. Checkpoint Data Storage:**
- Checkpoints stored on special `partio/checkpoints/v1` branch in GitHub
- Uses tree structure: `[shard]/[rest]/[version]/[file]`
- Files:
  - `metadata.json` - Checkpoint metadata
  - `0/full.jsonl` - Session transcript (one JSON per line)
  - `0/diff.patch` - Unified diff of changes
  - `0/plan.md` - Markdown plan file

**3. No Server-Side Caching:**
- Each API request hits GitHub API directly via Octokit
- Relies on GitHub API rate limiting (60 req/hour unauthenticated, 5000/hour authenticated)
- No database or Redis caching layer

---

### 6. Next.js Configuration

**next.config.ts:**
```typescript
{
  images: {
    remotePatterns: [{
      protocol: "https",
      hostname: "avatars.githubusercontent.com",  // Allow GitHub avatars
    }],
  },
}
```

**Simple config:** Only enables remote image loading from GitHub for user avatars.

---

### 7. Package Dependencies

**Core:**
- `next@16.1.6` - Framework
- `react@19.2.3` - UI library
- `react-dom@19.2.3`
- `typescript@5.9.3`

**Authentication & GitHub:**
- `next-auth@5.0.0-beta.25` - OAuth & session management
- `@octokit/rest@21.1.1` - GitHub API client

**Data Fetching:**
- `swr@2.3.3` - Client-side data fetching

**Styling:**
- `tailwindcss@4` - CSS utility framework
- `@tailwindcss/postcss@4`

**Content Rendering:**
- `react-markdown@10.1.0` - Markdown parser
- `remark-gfm@4.0.1` - GitHub Flavored Markdown support
- `react-syntax-highlighter@16.1.0` - Code block syntax highlighting
- `shiki@3.22.0` - Alternative syntax highlighter (installed but appears unused)
- `rehype-raw@7.0.0` - Allow raw HTML in markdown

**Animation:**
- `framer-motion@12.34.1`

**Utilities:**
- `date-fns@4.1.0` - Date formatting
- `clsx@2.1.1` - className utility

**Dev Tools:**
- `eslint@9`, `eslint-config-next@16.1.6`

---

### 8. Authentication & Middleware

**NextAuth Configuration (src/lib/auth.ts):**
```typescript
{
  providers: [GitHub({
    scopes: "repo read:user"  // Can read private repos and user info
  })],
  callbacks: {
    jwt: Store GitHub access_token and login in JWT
    session: Add accessToken & login to session object
  },
  pages: {
    signIn: "/login"  // Custom login page
  }
}
```

**Middleware (src/middleware.ts):**
```typescript
- Wraps all requests with auth()
- Redirects unauthenticated users to /login
- Redirects authenticated users away from /login to /
- Skips auth for: /api/auth/*, /_next/*, /favicon.ico, /partio-logo.svg
```

**Session Object:**
```typescript
session.user = {
  name, email, image,
  login  // GitHub username
}
session.accessToken  // GitHub OAuth token used for API calls
```

---

### 9. The Overview Page (/dashboard/page.tsx)

**Purpose:** Main dashboard with stats, activity heatmap, and recent activity table

**Components:**
1. **Greeting** - "Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening, [firstName]"
2. **OverviewStatsRow** - 4 stat cards:
   - Total Checkpoints (with active repo count)
   - This Week (with trend % vs previous week)
   - Avg AI Authorship (agent_percent average)
   - Active Streak (consecutive days with checkpoints)
3. **Heatmap** - GitHub-style 91-day activity visualization
   - 7 days per row, colored by intensity
   - Shows dates with checkpoint counts
   - Supports tooltips on hover
4. **RecentCommitsTable** - Table of 10 most recent checkpoints
   - Shows checkpoint ID, repo, created time
   - Clickable to detail page
5. **Empty State** - When no checkpoints exist

**Data Flow:**
```
useRecentCheckpoints() hook
  ↓
GET /api/github/repos/recent-commits
  ↓
- List repos with checkpoints via listReposWithCheckpoints()
- For each repo, fetch checkpoints via listCheckpoints()
- Collect all checkpoints & build heatmap (date → count)
- Calculate stats:
  - total_checkpoints
  - active_repos (unique repo count)
  - this_week (checkpoints in last 7 days)
  - prev_week (checkpoints in days 7-14)
  - avg_agent_percent (average of agent_percent field)
  - streak_days (consecutive days with activity)
- Enrich top 10 checkpoints with commit data
- Return: { items, heatmap, stats }
```

**Performance Notes:**
- Fetches ALL checkpoints across all repos to calculate stats
- Uses `Promise.allSettled` for fault tolerance
- Filters to top 10 for display
- Stat calculations happen server-side (API route)

---

### 10. Performance Optimizations

**Implemented:**

1. **Promise.allSettled:** Graceful handling of individual repo/checkpoint failures without blocking others

2. **Selective enrichment:** Recent checkpoints endpoint fetches all checkpoint metadata, but only enriches top 10 with commit details (2-pass approach)

3. **Deduplication:** SWR deduplicates requests within 30s window, preventing duplicate API calls

4. **Lazy loading:**
   - Checkpoint transcripts only loaded when "Sessions" tab active
   - Diffs loaded on "Files" tab open
   - Plans loaded on "Plan" tab open

5. **Message grouping:** TranscriptViewer merges consecutive tool-only messages to reduce DOM nodes

6. **Collapsible content:** Long assistant messages (500+ chars) collapsible to improve render performance

7. **Dynamic imports:** Components can be code-split (implicit via Next.js)

8. **Image optimization:** Remote GitHub avatars use Next.js Image component

9. **CSS variables:** Theme switching via CSS variables (no re-render needed)

**Not Implemented (optimization opportunities):**

1. **Server-side caching** - Every API call hits GitHub API fresh
2. **Pagination** - All checkpoints loaded at once (potential issue with many repos)
3. **Request batching** - No GraphQL, separate REST calls
4. **ISR/SSG** - Everything is CSR or SSR-on-demand
5. **Query parameter memoization** - Could optimize repo list queries

---

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/middleware.ts` | Auth guard for all routes |
| `src/lib/auth.ts` | NextAuth + GitHub setup |
| `src/app/(dashboard)/page.tsx` | Overview dashboard |
| `src/app/(dashboard)/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[checkpointId]/page.tsx` | Checkpoint detail (3-tab interface) |
| `src/lib/github/checkpoints.ts` | Fetch checkpoints from partio branch |
| `src/lib/github/session.ts` | Parse JSONL transcripts |
| `src/lib/github/diff.ts` | Fetch unified diffs |
| `src/lib/github/plan.ts` | Fetch markdown plans |
| `src/hooks/use-checkpoints.ts` | SWR hooks for checkpoint data |
| `src/components/ui/transcript-viewer.tsx` | Session rendering with collapsible messages |
| `src/components/ui/heatmap.tsx` | Activity grid visualization |
| `src/app/globals.css` | Tailwind config + CSS variables |

This is a well-structured Next.js application with clean separation of concerns, proper authentication flow, and a focused feature set around checkpoint visualization and browsing.

**Partio** is a Next.js 16 dashboard for browsing AI agent checkpoint data stored in GitHub repositories. It uses the `src/` directory layout with the App Router and requires GitHub OAuth authentication.

  ↓
- List repos with checkpoints via listReposWithCheckpoints()
- For each repo, fetch checkpoints via listCheckpoints()
- Collect all checkpoints & build heatmap (date → count)
- Calculate stats:
  - total_checkpoints
  - active_repos (unique repo count)
  - this_week (checkpoints in last 7 days)
  - prev_week (checkpoints in days 7-14)
  - avg_agent_percent (average of agent_percent field)
  - streak_days (consecutive days with activity)
- Enrich top 10 checkpoints with commit data
- Return: { items, heatmap, stats }
```

**Performance Notes:**
- Fetches ALL checkpoints across all repos to calculate stats
- Uses `Promise.allSettled` for fault tolerance
- Filters to top 10 for display
- Stat calculations happen server-side (API route)

---

### 10. Performance Optimizations

**Implemented:**

1. **Promise.allSettled:** Graceful handling of individual repo/checkpoint failures without blocking others

2. **Selective enrichment:** Recent checkpoints endpoint fetches all checkpoint metadata, but only enriches top 10 with commit details (2-pass approach)

3. **Deduplication:** SWR deduplicates requests within 30s window, preventing duplicate API calls

4. **Lazy loading:**
   - Checkpoint transcripts only loaded when "Sessions" tab active
   - Diffs loaded on "Files" tab open
   - Plans loaded on "Plan" tab open

5. **Message grouping:** TranscriptViewer merges consecutive tool-only messages to reduce DOM nodes

6. **Collapsible content:** Long assistant messages (500+ chars) collapsible to improve render performance

7. **Dynamic imports:** Components can be code-split (implicit via Next.js)

8. **Image optimization:** Remote GitHub avatars use Next.js Image component

9. **CSS variables:** Theme switching via CSS variables (no re-render needed)

**Not Implemented (optimization opportunities):**

1. **Server-side caching** - Every API call hits GitHub API fresh
2. **Pagination** - All checkpoints loaded at once (potential issue with many repos)
3. **Request batching** - No GraphQL, separate REST calls
4. **ISR/SSG** - Everything is CSR or SSR-on-demand
5. **Query parameter memoization** - Could optimize repo list queries

---

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/middleware.ts` | Auth guard for all routes |
| `src/lib/auth.ts` | NextAuth + GitHub setup |
| `src/app/(dashboard)/page.tsx` | Overview dashboard |
| `src/app/(dashboard)/[owner]/[repo]/checkpoints/[checkpointId]/page.tsx` | Checkpoint detail (3-tab interface) |
| `src/lib/github/checkpoints.ts` | Fetch checkpoints from partio branch |
| `src/lib/github/session.ts` | Parse JSONL transcripts |
| `src/lib/github/diff.ts` | Fetch unified diffs |
| `src/lib/github/plan.ts` | Fetch markdown plans |
| `src/hooks/use-checkpoints.ts` | SWR hooks for checkpoint data |
| `src/components/ui/transcript-viewer.tsx` | Session rendering with collapsible messages |
| `src/components/ui/heatmap.tsx` | Activity grid visualization |
| `src/app/globals.css` | Tailwind config + CSS variables |

This is a well-structured Next.js application with clean separation of concerns, proper authentication flow, and a focused feature set around checkpoint visualization and browsing.

            cache_control = h['value']
        elif name == 'etag':
            etag = h['value'][:30]
        elif name == 'expires':
            expires = h['value']
        elif name == 'vary':
            vary = h['value']
    
    status = e['response']['status']
    print(f'\n{short_url}')
    print(f'  Status: {status}')
    print(f'  Cache-Control: {cache_control or \"(none)\"}')
    if etag: print(f'  ETag: {etag}')
    if expires: print(f'  Expires: {expires}')
    if vary: print(f'  Vary: {vary}')

import json

    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("7. DNS / TLS / CONNECTION OVERHEAD")
print("=" * 80)

# Group by connection ID
connections = {}
for e in entries:
    conn_id = e.get('connection', e.get('_connectionId', 'unknown'))
    timings = e.get('timings', {})
    url = e['request']['url']
    
    # Extract host
    from urllib.parse import urlparse
    host = urlparse(url).netloc
    
    if conn_id not in connections:
        connections[conn_id] = {
            'host': host,
            'count': 0,
            'dns': timings.get('dns', -1),
            'connect': timings.get('connect', -1),
            'ssl': timings.get('ssl', -1),
        }
    connections[conn_id]['count'] += 1

print(f"\nTotal unique connections: {len(connections)}")
print(f"\n{'ConnID':>8} {'Host':>45} {'Reqs':>5} {'DNS':>8} {'Connect':>8} {'SSL':>8}")
print("-" * 90)
for conn_id, info in sorted(connections.items()):
    dns = info['dns'] if info['dns'] >= 0 else -1
    connect = info['connect'] if info['connect'] >= 0 else -1
    ssl = info['ssl'] if info['ssl'] >= 0 else -1
    dns_s = f"{dns:.0f}ms" if dns >= 0 else "cached"
    connect_s = f"{connect:.0f}ms" if connect >= 0 else "reused"
    ssl_s = f"{ssl:.0f}ms" if ssl >= 0 else "reused"
    print(f"{conn_id:>8} {info['host']:>45} {info['count']:>5} {dns_s:>8} {connect_s:>8} {ssl_s:>8}")

# Check for repeated DNS/connect
print("\n\nRequests with fresh DNS resolution:")
for e in entries:
    timings = e.get('timings', {})
    if timings.get('dns', -1) > 0:
        url = e['request']['url']
        if len(url) > 80:
            url = url[:77] + '...'
        print(f"  dns={timings['dns']:.0f}ms connect={timings.get('connect', -1):.0f}ms ssl={timings.get('ssl', -1):.0f}ms | {url}")

print("\nRequests with fresh connection (connect > 0):")
for e in entries:
    timings = e.get('timings', {})
    if timings.get('connect', -1) > 0:
        url = e['request']['url']
        if len(url) > 80:
            url = url[:77] + '...'
        print(f"  connect={timings['connect']:.0f}ms ssl={timings.get('ssl', -1):.0f}ms | {url}")

import json

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("8. CACHING HEADERS ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)

for e in sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('startedDateTime', '')):
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 60:
        url = url[:57] + '...'
    
    headers = e.get('response', {}).get('headers', [])
    cache_control = None
    etag = None
    expires = None
    age = None
    vary = None
    
    for h in headers:
        name = h.get('name', '').lower()
        if name == 'cache-control':
            cache_control = h.get('value', '')
        elif name == 'etag':
            etag = h.get('value', '')
        elif name == 'expires':
            expires = h.get('value', '')
        elif name == 'age':
            age = h.get('value', '')
        elif name == 'vary':
            vary = h.get('value', '')
    
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    status = e.get('response', {}).get('status', 0)
    
    cc_display = cache_control if cache_control else '(none)'
    if len(cc_display) > 50:
        cc_display = cc_display[:47] + '...'
    
    print(f"\n  {rtype:10} {status:3d} | {url}")
    print(f"    Cache-Control: {cc_display}")
    if etag:
        print(f"    ETag: {etag[:50]}")
    if age:
        print(f"    Age: {age}")

PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("9. COMPRESSION ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)

print(f"\n{'Type':>10} {'Status':>6} {'ContentEnc':>15} {'ContentSize':>12} {'MimeType':>35} URL")
print("-" * 140)

for e in sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0), reverse=True):
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 45:
        url = url[:42] + '...'
    
    headers = e.get('response', {}).get('headers', [])
    content_encoding = None
    transfer_encoding = None
    
    for h in headers:
        name = h.get('name', '').lower()
        if name == 'content-encoding':
            content_encoding = h.get('value', '')
        elif name == 'transfer-encoding':
            transfer_encoding = h.get('value', '')
    
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    status = e.get('response', {}).get('status', 0)
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    mime = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('mimeType', 'unknown')
    
    enc = content_encoding if content_encoding else '(none)'
    
    print(f"{rtype:>10} {status:>6} {enc:>15} {size/1024:>10.1f}KB {mime:>35} {url}")

# Summary
compressed = 0
uncompressed = 0
for e in entries:
    headers = e.get('response', {}).get('headers', [])
    has_encoding = False
    for h in headers:
        if h.get('name', '').lower() == 'content-encoding':
            has_encoding = True
            break
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    if has_encoding:
        compressed += 1
    else:
        uncompressed += 1

print(f"\nSummary: {compressed} responses compressed, {uncompressed} uncompressed")

# Check Accept-Encoding in requests
print("\nClient Accept-Encoding header (first request):")
for h in entries[0].get('request', {}).get('headers', []):
    if h.get('name', '').lower() == 'accept-encoding':
        print(f"  {h['value']}")

PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
from datetime import datetime

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("10. BLOCKING RESOURCES & RENDER-BLOCKING ANALYSIS")
print("=" * 80)

# Check priority and initiator for render-blocking resources
sorted_entries = sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('startedDateTime', ''))
base_time = datetime.fromisoformat(sorted_entries[0]['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000

print("\nResources loaded before first API call (likely render-blocking):")
print(f"{'#':>3} {'Start':>8} {'Duration':>8} {'Priority':>12} {'Type':>10} {'Size':>8} URL")
print("-" * 110)

first_api_time = None
for e in sorted_entries:
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    if rtype == 'fetch':
        ts = datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000
        first_api_time = ts - base_time
        break

for i, e in enumerate(sorted_entries):
    ts = datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000
    offset = ts - base_time
    if offset > (first_api_time or 9999):
        break
    
    time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 50:
        url = url[:47] + '...'
    size = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {}).get('size', 0)
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    priority = e.get('_priority', 'unknown')
    
    print(f"{i+1:3d} {offset:7.0f}ms {time_ms:7.0f}ms {priority:>12} {rtype:>10} {size/1024:7.1f}KB {url}")

print(f"\nFirst API call starts at: {first_api_time:.0f}ms")

# Check for blocking JS in the <head> that isn't async/defer
print("\n\nInitiator analysis for JS bundles:")
for e in sorted_entries:
    if e.get('_resourceType') == 'script':
        url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
        fname = url.split('/')[-1].split('?')[0]
        initiator = e.get('_initiator', {})
        init_type = initiator.get('type', 'unknown')
        priority = e.get('_priority', 'unknown')
        ts = datetime.fromisoformat(e['startedDateTime'].replace('Z', '+00:00')).timestamp() * 1000
        offset = ts - base_time
        time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
        print(f"  {offset:6.0f}ms | {time_ms:5.0f}ms | pri={priority:>8} | init={init_type:>8} | {fname}")

print("\n\nRSC (React Server Component) Fetch Pattern:")
rsc_fetches = [e for e in entries if e.get('_resourceType') == 'fetch' and '_rsc=' in e['request']['url']]
print(f"  Total RSC fetches: {len(rsc_fetches)}")
rsc_routes = {}
for e in rsc_fetches:
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '').split('?')[0]
    if url not in rsc_routes:
        rsc_routes[url] = 0
    rsc_routes[url] += 1
for route, count in sorted(rsc_routes.items()):
    print(f"    {route}: {count} fetches")

PYEOF

python3 -c "
import json, base64
with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)
entries = har['log']['entries']

# 11. Features detected - analyze all API responses and HTML
print('=== 11. FEATURES DETECTED IN OVERVIEW PAGE ===')

# Auth/User
for e in entries:
    if '/auth/me' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            print(f'\n1. USER PROFILE: {json.dumps(data, indent=2)}')

# Checkpoints (activity/contributions)
for e in entries:
    if '/checkpoints' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            print(f'\n2. CHECKPOINTS (Activity): {len(data.get(\"checkpoints\", []))} items, timeframe={data.get(\"timeframe\")}')
            for cp in data.get('checkpoints', [])[:3]:
                print(f'   - {cp.get(\"commit_message\", \"\")} on {cp.get(\"repo_full_name\", \"\")} by {cp.get(\"agent\", \"\")} ({cp.get(\"additions\", 0)}+ {cp.get(\"deletions\", 0)}-)')

# Repos
for e in entries:
    if '/cache/repos' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            repos = data.get('repos', [])
            languages = set(r.get('language') for r in repos if r.get('language'))
            private_count = sum(1 for r in repos if r.get('private'))
            public_count = sum(1 for r in repos if not r.get('private'))
            with_checkpoints = sum(1 for r in repos if r.get('checkpoint_count', 0) > 0)
            print(f'\n3. REPOS: {len(repos)} total ({private_count} private, {public_count} public)')
            print(f'   With checkpoints: {with_checkpoints}')
            print(f'   Languages: {sorted(languages)}')
            print(f'   Repo fields: {list(repos[0].keys())}')

# Sync status
for e in entries:
    if '/sync-status' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            print(f'\n4. SYNC STATUS: {json.dumps(data)}')

# Status page
for e in entries:
    if 'status.entire.io' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            print(f'\n5. SERVICE STATUS: {json.dumps(data, indent=2)}')

# PostHog analytics
print(f'\n6. ANALYTICS: PostHog (42.entire.io) with session recording, surveys, feature flags, dead-click autocapture')

# Surveys
for e in entries:
    if '/surveys' in e['request']['url'] and '42.entire' in e['request']['url']:
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        if text:
            data = json.loads(text)
            surveys = data.get('surveys', [])
            print(f'\n7. SURVEYS: {len(surveys)} active surveys')
            for s in surveys[:3]:
                print(f'   - {s.get(\"name\", \"\")} (type: {s.get(\"type\", \"\")})')
" 2>&1

python3 -c "
import json, base64
with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)
entries = har['log']['entries']

# 10. Render-blocking resources - check initial HTML for blocking scripts/styles
print('=== 10. RENDER-BLOCKING RESOURCES ===')
for e in entries:
    if e['request']['url'] == 'https://entire.io/overview':
        content = e.get('response', {}).get('content', {})
        text = content.get('text', '')
        enc = content.get('encoding', '')
        if enc == 'base64':
            text = base64.b64decode(text).decode()
        
        # Look for script and link tags
        import re
        scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>', text)
        links = re.findall(r'<link[^>]*>', text)
        
        print('Scripts in HTML:')
        for s in scripts:
            print(f'  {s}')
        print(f'\nLink tags in HTML:')
        for l in links:
            print(f'  {l}')
        
        # Check if SPA (React/Vue)
        if 'id=\"root\"' in text or 'id=\"app\"' in text or 'id=\"__next\"' in text:
            print(f'\nSPA detected (client-side rendered)')
        
        print(f'\nHTML size: {len(text)} bytes')
        # Show a snippet
        print(f'\nHTML head snippet:')
        head_match = re.search(r'<head>(.*?)</head>', text, re.DOTALL)
        if head_match:
            print(head_match.group(0)[:2000])
" 2>&1

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json
from datetime import datetime

with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/app.partio.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)

entries = har['log']['entries']

print("=" * 80)
print("ADDITIONAL: BLOCKED TIME ANALYSIS (queue/stall time)")
print("=" * 80)

print("\nRequests with significant blocked/queuing time (>100ms):")
for e in sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('timings', {}).get('blocked', 0), reverse=True):
    timings = e.get('timings', {})
    blocked = timings.get('blocked', 0)
    queuing = timings.get('_blocked_queueing', 0)
    if blocked > 100:
        url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
        fname = url.split('/')[-1].split('?')[0]
        time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
        wait = timings.get('wait', 0)
        print(f"  blocked={blocked:.0f}ms (queuing={queuing:.0f}ms) wait={wait:.0f}ms total={time_ms:.0f}ms | {fname[:50]}")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("ADDITIONAL: DUPLICATE/REDUNDANT REQUESTS")
print("=" * 80)

# Check for duplicate URLs
url_counts = {}
for e in entries:
    url = e['request']['url']
    path = url.replace('https://app.partio.io', '').split('?')[0]
    if path not in url_counts:
        url_counts[path] = []
    url_counts[path].append(e)

print("\nURLs requested multiple times:")
for path, es in sorted(url_counts.items()):
    if len(es) > 1:
        total_time = sum(e.get('time', 0) for e in es)
        print(f"\n  {path} -- {len(es)} requests, total {total_time:.0f}ms")
        for e in es:
            qs = e['request']['url'].split('?')[-1] if '?' in e['request']['url'] else ''
            time_ms = e.get('time', 0)
            print(f"    {time_ms:.0f}ms | ?{qs[:60]}")

print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("ADDITIONAL: SERVER RESPONSE TIME (TTFB / wait time)")
print("=" * 80)

print("\nTop TTFB (Time To First Byte = wait time):")
for e in sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.get('timings', {}).get('wait', 0), reverse=True)[:10]:
    url = e['request']['url'].replace('https://app.partio.io', '')
    if len(url) > 60:
        url = url[:57] + '...'
    wait = e.get('timings', {}).get('wait', 0)
    total = e.get('time', 0)
    rtype = e.get('_resourceType', '?')
    print(f"  wait={wait:7.0f}ms (total={total:7.0f}ms) | {rtype:>10} | {url}")

PYEOF

import json
from datetime import datetime
with open('/Users/arvos/Downloads/entire.io.har', 'r') as f:
    har = json.load(f)
entries = har['log']['entries']

# 3. Parallelization analysis
print('=== 3. REQUEST CHAIN / PARALLELIZATION ANALYSIS ===')
print()

# Parse all timestamps
first_start = None
timeline = []
for e in entries:
    start_str = e.get('startedDateTime', '')
    if 'Z' in start_str:
        start_str = start_str.replace('Z', '+00:00')
    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(start_str)
    if first_start is None:
        first_start = dt
    rel_start = (dt - first_start).total_seconds() * 1000
    rel_end = rel_start + e.get('time', 0)
    url = e['request']['url']
    timeline.append((rel_start, rel_end, url, e.get('time', 0)))

# Identify sequential chains
print('Phase 1: Initial HTML (0-70ms)')
print('  -> GET /overview [70ms]')
print()
print('Phase 2: Critical assets loaded in parallel (~73-261ms)')
print('  -> JS bundle + CSS + PostHog beacon (parallel)')
print()
print('Phase 3: After JS execution (~418-700ms)')
print('  API calls and PostHog scripts loaded in parallel:')

api_calls = []
for start, end, url, dur in timeline:
    if '/api/' in url and 'entire.io' in url:
        short = url.split('entire.io')[1].split('?')[0]
        api_calls.append((start, end, short, dur))
        
for start, end, url, dur in sorted(api_calls):
    print(f'    [{start:.0f}ms -> {end:.0f}ms] ({dur:.0f}ms) {url}')

print()
print('Sequential dependencies detected:')
print('  1. HTML (0ms) -> JS bundle (76ms) -> API calls (~418ms)')
print('     The SPA must download+parse 1.8MB JS before making ANY API call')
print('  2. github.com/jcleira.png (654ms) -> 302 redirect -> avatars.githubusercontent.com (1000ms)')
print('     GitHub avatar requires a redirect hop adding ~347ms')
print()
print('Parallelization opportunities:')
print('  - The 4 API calls at ~649ms ARE already parallelized (good)')
print('  - /auth/me starts at 418ms, other APIs at ~649ms')
print('  - Consider SSR or preloading API data to avoid waiting for JS parse')
print('  - The avatar URL could be pre-resolved to avoid the redirect')

# Concurrency analysis
print()
print('Max concurrent requests:')
for t in range(0, 1200, 50):
    active = sum(1 for s, e, _, _ in timeline if s <= t < e)
    if active > 0:
        print(f'  {t}ms: {active} concurrent requests')
" 2>&1

Now I have all the data. Here is the comprehensive performance analysis.

---

# Performance Analysis: app.partio.io

## Overview

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total Requests | 38 |
| Total Content Size | 1,029 KB (uncompressed) |
| Wall-Clock Load Time | 4,196 ms (4.2 seconds) |
| Cumulative Request Time | 12,361 ms |

The app is a **Next.js application** (using Turbopack and React Server Components) deployed on what appears to be **Vercel** (based on `dpl_` deployment hashes).

---

## CRITICAL BOTTLENECK #1: `/api/github/repos/recent-commits` (3,261 ms)

This is by far the single worst offender. This one API call accounts for **78% of the wall-clock load time**.

- **URL**: `/api/github/repos/recent-commits`
- **Wait (TTFB)**: 3,258 ms -- nearly the entire time is spent waiting for the server to respond
- **Response size**: Only 6.6 KB
- **Started at**: 870 ms into page load
- **Finished at**: 4,131 ms -- this is what defines the end of page load

The server is spending over 3 seconds computing or fetching this data. Since the response is only 6.6 KB, this is clearly a **backend performance problem**, not a transfer problem. The server is likely making sequential GitHub API calls to fetch recent commits across multiple repositories.

**Recommendations:**
- Cache this response server-side (Redis/in-memory) with a 30-60 second TTL -- commit data does not need to be real-time
- If fetching from multiple repos, parallelize the GitHub API calls on the backend
- Consider using a GitHub webhook to maintain a pre-computed list of recent commits rather than computing on-demand
- Return a loading skeleton immediately and fetch this data client-side after the page is interactive
- Add a stale-while-revalidate caching strategy so users see cached data instantly while fresh data loads in the background

---

## CRITICAL BOTTLENECK #2: Excessive RSC (React Server Component) Fetches

There are **13 RSC fetch requests** to just 3 routes, each fetched 4-5 times with different `_rsc` query parameters:

| Route | Fetch Count | Total Time |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| `/` | 4 fetches | 664 ms |
| `/settings` | 5 fetches | 615 ms |
| `/repositories` | 4 fetches | 767 ms |

These represent Next.js App Router RSC payloads being fetched multiple times as the component tree hydrates. Each fetch returns a small `text/x-component` payload (0.7-3.0 KB), but the cumulative server wait time across all 13 requests is **2,046 ms**.

**Recommendations:**
- Investigate why each route is being fetched 4-5 times -- this likely indicates unnecessary re-renders or re-fetches during hydration
- Use Next.js `loading.tsx` boundaries to avoid waterfall fetches
- Consider whether the `/settings` route data needs to be prefetched on the home page at all (5 fetches to `/settings` during initial page load seems excessive)
- Use `React.cache()` or Next.js `unstable_cache` to deduplicate identical data fetches

---

## BOTTLENECK #3: Duplicate `/api/auth/session` Calls

The auth session endpoint is called **twice** (252 ms + 357 ms = 609 ms total). Both return identical 0.2 KB responses. This is wasted time.

**Recommendations:**
- Deduplicate auth session checks -- use a shared context/provider or React cache
- Store the session result in a React context after the first fetch

---

## BOTTLENECK #4: JavaScript Bundle Blocking / Queuing

**14 JavaScript chunks** are loaded, totaling **519.7 KB** (uncompressed). While all are marked `Low` priority and loaded via the parser, they exhibit a consistent **~215 ms blocked/queuing time** each. This suggests the browser is hitting HTTP/2 multiplexing limits or the CDN is slow to begin responding.

The worst offender is `d8a183ded7b46d92.js` at **738 ms** (216 ms blocked + 521 ms TTFB for just 28.3 KB).

Key JS bundles by size:
| Bundle | Size |
|--------|------|
| `aebc3b6d791f68d2.js` | 219.2 KB |
| `999fca0429daf9c4.js` | 108.5 KB |
| `82abf2d65f5428ae.js` | 32.5 KB |
| All others (11 chunks) | 159.9 KB |

**Recommendations:**
- Audit the 219 KB and 108 KB bundles for tree-shaking opportunities -- these likely contain large libraries (e.g., a UI framework, date library, or charting library)
- Use dynamic imports (`next/dynamic`) for components not needed on initial render
- Consider whether all 14 chunks are needed for the initial page
- The 2 late-loaded chunks (`89e1b92d65ef0347.js` at 1,344 ms and `3dddf3d9533aa342.js` at 1,467 ms) are loaded via script initiator, meaning they are dynamically imported -- this is good practice and should be extended to more bundles

---

## BOTTLENECK #5: Large Uncompressed Image (340 KB)

A **340 KB PNG image** is loaded from `raw.githubusercontent.com`:
```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catlee/omarchy-dracula-theme/main/background.png
```

This appears to be a theme background image served without compression (no `content-encoding` header) and with only a 5-minute cache (`max-age=300`).

**Recommendations:**
- Host this image on your own CDN and convert to WebP or AVIF (could reduce to ~50-100 KB)
- Use Next.js `<Image>` component with optimization
- Add `loading="lazy"` if it's below the fold
- Serve through `/_next/image` endpoint for automatic optimization

---

## What Is Working Well

1. **Compression**: 28 of 38 responses use **Brotli compression** (`br`) -- this is excellent. The uncompressed items are either already-compressed formats (woff2, webp) or very small responses.

2. **Static asset caching**: All JS, CSS, and font files have `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` -- perfect immutable caching for content-hashed assets.

3. **Connection reuse**: All 38 requests share a single connection (connection ID 443). No wasted DNS/TLS handshakes. The app benefits from being served entirely from one origin.

4. **Font loading**: Two woff2 fonts (86.8 KB total) load early and fast (60-87 ms). They are properly compressed at the format level.

5. **CSS is minimal**: Only one 39.7 KB CSS file -- well-bundled.

6. **Next.js Image optimization**: Avatar images are served through `/_next/image` as WebP at tiny sizes (0.6-0.9 KB).

---

## Caching Concerns

The RSC fetch responses and the document have `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate` but show very high `Age` headers (up to **404,009 seconds / ~4.7 days**). This means:
- Vercel's edge cache is serving stale responses
- The `max-age=0, must-revalidate` should force revalidation but the high `Age` suggests the CDN is caching aggressively despite this header
- This could mean users are seeing stale data for the `/settings` and `/repositories` RSC payloads

**Recommendation**: Investigate whether the Vercel caching behavior matches expectations. If data must be fresh, use `private, no-cache` or `s-maxage=0` to prevent CDN caching.

---

## Summary: Prioritized Action Items

| Priority | Issue | Impact | Effort |
|----------|-------|--------|--------|
| **P0** | `/api/github/repos/recent-commits` takes 3.2s | Saves ~3s of load time | Medium -- add server-side caching |
| **P0** | 13 redundant RSC fetches across 3 routes | Saves ~1-2s of server time | Medium -- investigate hydration pattern |
| **P1** | Duplicate `/api/auth/session` calls | Saves ~350ms | Low -- deduplicate with React cache |
| **P1** | 340 KB unoptimized PNG from GitHub | Saves ~200ms + bandwidth | Low -- use Next.js Image with local copy |
| **P2** | JS bundle audit (327 KB in top 2 chunks) | Reduces parse/eval time | Medium -- analyze with bundle analyzer |
| **P2** | Stale CDN cache (Age: 4.7 days with max-age=0) | Data freshness issue | Low -- review Vercel cache headers |
| **P3** | ~215ms consistent queuing on all JS chunks | Improves parallelism | Low -- may be CDN/origin issue |

Fixing the top two issues alone (the slow API endpoint and redundant RSC fetches) could reduce perceived page load time from **4.2 seconds to under 1.5 seconds**.

The RSC fetch responses and the document have `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate` but show very high `Age` headers (up to **404,009 seconds / ~4.7 days**). This means:
- Vercel's edge cache is serving stale responses
- The `max-age=0, must-revalidate` should force revalidation but the high `Age` suggests the CDN is caching aggressively despite this header
- This could mean users are seeing stale data for the `/settings` and `/repositories` RSC payloads

**Recommendation**: Investigate whether the Vercel caching behavior matches expectations. If data must be fresh, use `private, no-cache` or `s-maxage=0` to prevent CDN caching.

---

## Summary: Prioritized Action Items

| Priority | Issue | Impact | Effort |
|----------|-------|--------|--------|
| **P0** | `/api/github/repos/recent-commits` takes 3.2s | Saves ~3s of load time | Medium -- add server-side caching |
| **P0** | 13 redundant RSC fetches across 3 routes | Saves ~1-2s of server time | Medium -- investigate hydration pattern |
| **P1** | Duplicate `/api/auth/session` calls | Saves ~350ms | Low -- deduplicate with React cache |
| **P1** | 340 KB unoptimized PNG from GitHub | Saves ~200ms + bandwidth | Low -- use Next.js Image with local copy |
| **P2** | JS bundle audit (327 KB in top 2 chunks) | Reduces parse/eval time | Medium -- analyze with bundle analyzer |
| **P2** | Stale CDN cache (Age: 4.7 days with max-age=0) | Data freshness issue | Low -- review Vercel cache headers |
| **P3** | ~215ms consistent queuing on all JS chunks | Improves parallelism | Low -- may be CDN/origin issue |

Fixing the top two issues alone (the slow API endpoint and redundant RSC fetches) could reduce perceived page load time from **4.2 seconds to under 1.5 seconds**.

Now I have all the data. Here is the comprehensive analysis.

---

## Entire.io Overview Page -- Performance Analysis

### 1. Page Load Summary

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Total requests** | 23 |
| **Page onLoad** | **1,058ms** |
| **DOMContentLoaded** | **345ms** |
| **Total content size (uncompressed)** | 2,697 KB (2.6 MB) |
| **Total transfer size (compressed)** | ~936 KB |
| **HTTP methods** | 20 GET, 3 POST |
| **Status codes** | 20x200, 1x302, 2x0 (beacon/cancelled) |
| **Hosts contacted** | 5 (entire.io, 42.entire.io, status.entire.io, github.com, avatars.githubusercontent.com) |

### 2. Top 10 Slowest Requests

| Rank | Request | Time | Size | Bottleneck |
|------|---------|------|------|------------|
| 1 | `GET /api/v1/cache/repos` | **570ms** | 176.5 KB | **Wait: 426ms**, Receive: 143ms |
| 2 | `GET github.com/jcleira.png` (302 redirect) | **347ms** | 0 KB | New TLS connection: 185ms + wait: 191ms |
| 3 | `GET /api/v1/cache/sync-status` | **315ms** | 51 bytes | Wait: 313ms (tiny response, slow server) |
| 4 | `GET 42.entire.io/api/surveys/` | **286ms** | 1.3 KB | Wait: 284ms |
| 5 | `GET /api/v1/cache/user/checkpoints` | **227ms** | 480 bytes | Wait: 225ms |
| 6 | `GET /api/v1/auth/me` | **211ms** | 186 bytes | Wait: 209ms |
| 7 | `GET posthog-recorder.js` | **208ms** | 246.5 KB | Wait: 104ms, Receive: 102ms |
| 8 | `POST /flags/` (PostHog) | **200ms** | 403 bytes | Wait: 196ms |
| 9 | `GET index-CzRXaeDO.js` (main bundle) | **185ms** | 1,802 KB | Wait: 63ms, Receive: 115ms |
| 10 | `GET surveys.js` (PostHog) | **166ms** | 88 KB | Wait: 163ms |

### 3. Request Waterfall and Sequential Dependencies

```
Timeline:
 0ms    HTML document (70ms)
 73ms   PostHog beacons (fire and forget)
 76ms   JS bundle (185ms) + CSS (81ms) -- parallel
         ---- JS PARSING GAP (~160ms) ----
 418ms  /auth/me (211ms) -- first API call after JS parse
         ---- 230ms gap before other APIs ----
 649ms  /checkpoints (227ms) + /repos (570ms) + /sync-status (315ms) + /status/summary (72ms) -- parallel burst
 654ms  github.com avatar (347ms redirect + 59ms fetch = 406ms total)
 1059ms Page fully loaded
```

**Critical path**: HTML (70ms) -> JS download (185ms) -> JS parse (~160ms) -> /auth/me (211ms) -> other APIs -> /repos finishes at 1220ms

**Key observations**:
- The core API calls (checkpoints, repos, sync-status, status) fire in parallel at ~649ms -- this is good
- But `/auth/me` fires 230ms earlier at 418ms. The other APIs likely wait for auth to resolve before firing, creating an **authentication waterfall**
- The `/cache/repos` endpoint is the critical bottleneck at 570ms, finishing last at 1220ms (after onLoad)
- The GitHub avatar redirect (`github.com -> avatars.githubusercontent.com`) adds an unnecessary 347ms hop

### 4. Large Responses

| Resource | Uncompressed | Compressed | Notes |
|----------|-------------|------------|-------|
| `index-CzRXaeDO.js` | **1,802 KB** | 496 KB | **Massive single JS bundle** |
| `posthog-recorder.js` | 246.5 KB | 88.4 KB | Session recording library |
| `/api/v1/cache/repos` | **176.5 KB** | 20.5 KB | **501 repos returned** |
| `index-B6J39GCp.css` | 92.4 KB | 16.5 KB | Single CSS file |
| `entire-mono-bold.woff2` | 92.4 KB | 92.4 KB | Font (already compressed) |
| `entire-mono-regular.woff2` | 90.0 KB | 90.0 KB | Font (already compressed) |
| `surveys.js` | 88.0 KB | 31.9 KB | PostHog surveys |

**The JS bundle at 1.8 MB uncompressed (496 KB transferred) is the single biggest concern.** There is no code splitting -- the entire app ships in one file.

**The repos endpoint returning 501 repos (176.5 KB) is the second major concern.** This is the overview page -- it almost certainly does not render all 501 repos. Pagination or limiting to visible repos would cut this dramatically.

### 5. API Endpoints and Timing

| Endpoint | Method | Time | Size | Purpose |
|----------|--------|------|------|---------|
| `/api/v1/auth/me` | GET | 211ms | 186 B | User authentication/profile |
| `/api/v1/cache/user/checkpoints?timeframe=last-month&limit=500` | GET | 227ms | 480 B | Activity/contribution data |
| `/api/v1/cache/repos` | GET | **570ms** | **176.5 KB** | All 501 repos with metadata |
| `/api/v1/cache/sync-status` | GET | 315ms | 51 B | Git sync status |
| `status.entire.io/api/v1/summary` | GET | 72ms | 143 B | Service health status |
| `42.entire.io/api/surveys/` | GET | 286ms | 1.3 KB | PostHog surveys |
| `42.entire.io/flags/` | POST | 200ms | 403 B | PostHog feature flags |

All API endpoints use the `/api/v1/cache/` prefix pattern, suggesting a caching layer (Redis or similar) sits between the client and the database/GitHub API.

### 6. Asset Loading

**JavaScript (6 files, 2,163 KB uncompressed)**:
- `index-CzRXaeDO.js` -- 1,802 KB (496 KB transferred, brotli). **Single monolithic bundle. No code splitting.**
- PostHog scripts (5 files) -- 361 KB uncompressed total. These are lazy-loaded after main bundle.

**CSS (1 file, 92.4 KB uncompressed)**:
- `index-B6J39GCp.css` -- 92.4 KB (16.5 KB transferred, brotli). Single CSS file -- reasonable size.

**Fonts (3 files, 234 KB)**:
- `entire-headline-semibold.woff2` -- 51.4 KB
- `entire-mono-regular.woff2` -- 90.0 KB
- `entire-mono-bold.woff2` -- 92.4 KB
- Fonts are NOT preloaded in the HTML -- they load after CSS parsing, causing potential FOUT (Flash of Unstyled Text)
- woff2 is already compressed so no additional encoding is applied (correct)

**Images (2 files, 27.5 KB)**:
- User avatar from GitHub -- 26.7 KB
- Favicon SVG -- 0.8 KB

### 7. DNS/TLS/Connection Overhead

| Host | Requests | New Connection? | Overhead |
|------|----------|----------------|----------|
| entire.io | 11 | No (reused) | 0ms |
| 42.entire.io | 9 | No (reused) | 0ms |
| status.entire.io | 1 | No (reused) | 0ms |
| github.com | 1 | **Yes** | DNS 1.5ms + TCP 123ms + TLS 61ms = **186ms** |
| avatars.githubusercontent.com | 1 | No (reused from github.com) | 0ms |

**Connection reuse is excellent** across entire.io subdomains. The only new connection is to github.com for the avatar redirect, which adds 186ms overhead.

### 8. Caching Headers

| Resource | Cache-Control | Analysis |
|----------|--------------|----------|
| HTML (`/overview`) | `public, max-age=0, must-revalidate` | Always revalidated -- correct for SPA shell |
| JS bundle | `public, max-age=14400` (4 hours) | Content-hashed filename enables cache busting -- but 4h is short. Could be `max-age=31536000` (1 year) since filename changes on deploy |
| CSS | `public, max-age=14400` (4 hours) | Same as JS -- could be much longer with hash |
| Fonts | `public, max-age=14400` (4 hours) | Should be `immutable, max-age=31536000` -- fonts rarely change |
| API endpoints (`/auth/me`, `/cache/repos`, etc.) | **(none)** | **No caching headers on API responses** -- missed opportunity |
| PostHog scripts | `max-age=60` | Very short, revalidated often |

**Major issue**: The content-hashed assets (JS, CSS, fonts) only have 4-hour cache. Since filenames change on deploy, these could safely be cached for 1 year with `immutable`.

### 9. Compression

| Resource | Encoding | Compression Ratio | Notes |
|----------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| JS bundle | **Brotli** | 28% (72% savings) | Excellent |
| CSS | **Brotli** | 18% (82% savings) | Excellent |
| `/cache/repos` | **Brotli** | 12% (88% savings) | Very good (JSON compresses well) |
| PostHog scripts | **gzip** | 36-40% | Good but brotli would be better |
| Fonts | None | N/A | woff2 is pre-compressed |
| API (small responses) | Brotli | >100% (overhead) | Compression adds overhead on tiny payloads |

Entire.io uses **Brotli** for its own resources and **gzip** for PostHog CDN resources. Compression is well-configured overall.

### 10. Render-Blocking Resources

The HTML document at `/overview` is a **minimal 1.7 KB SPA shell**:
```html
<script type="module" crossorigin src="/assets/index-CzRXaeDO.js">
<link rel="stylesheet" crossorigin href="/assets/index-B6J39GCp.css">
```

This is a **client-side rendered (CSR) SPA** with `<div id="root">`. This means:
- The browser downloads 1.7 KB HTML
- Then downloads and parses the **1.8 MB JS bundle** (496 KB compressed)
- Only THEN can any content render or API calls fire
- Time to first meaningful paint is at minimum ~418ms (when first API call fires)
- True content display likely ~700-1200ms (after APIs return)

**No server-side rendering (SSR)** is used. The entire app bootstraps client-side.

### 11. Features Detected on the Overview Page

Based on API responses and data structures:

| Feature | Data Source | Details |
|---------|------------|---------|
| **User profile** | `/auth/me` | GitHub login, admin status, discovery setting |
| **Repository list** | `/cache/repos` | **501 repos** with name, description, language, stars, privacy, checkpoint_count |
| **Checkpoints (AI sessions)** | `/cache/user/checkpoints` | Commit-linked AI agent sessions with token counts, agent name, additions/deletions |
| **Sync status** | `/cache/sync-status` | Real-time sync progress indicator |
| **Service health** | `status.entire.io/api/v1/summary` | Incident/maintenance status banner |
| **GitHub avatar** | `github.com/{username}.png` | Profile picture via GitHub redirect |
| **PostHog analytics** | `42.entire.io` (self-hosted PostHog) | Session recording, feature flags, surveys, dead-click tracking, exception autocapture |
| **User surveys** | PostHog surveys API | 1 active "Open feedback" survey |
| **Custom fonts** | Self-hosted woff2 | "Entire Headline Semibold", "Entire Mono Regular", "Entire Mono Bold" |

**Checkpoint data structure** (the core feature):
```json
{
  "checkpoint_id": "dbeb360f50ab",
  "commit_sha": "911f89c...",
  "commit_message": "Updated README",
  "commit_date": "2026-02-27T10:02:24.000Z",
  "additions": 5, "deletions": 1, "files_changed": 2,
  "repo_full_name": "partio-io/cli",
  "branch": "partio-change-branch",
  "agent": "Claude Code",
  "steps": 1, "session_count": 1,
  "input_tokens": 16, "output_tokens": 2470
}
```

**Repo data structure**:
```json
{
  "name": "cli",
  "full_name": "partio-io/cli",
  "description": "...",
  "html_url": "https://github.com/partio-io/cli",
  "stargazers_count": 0,
  "language": "Go",
  "private": false,
  "updated_at": "2026-02-27T10:02:33.000Z",
  "default_branch": "main",
  "permission": "read",
  "checkpoint_count": 1
}
```

---

## Summary of Performance Issues (ranked by impact)

1. **Monolithic 1.8 MB JS bundle (496 KB compressed)** -- No code splitting. The entire application ships in one file. This delays Time to Interactive and blocks all API calls. Code splitting by route would dramatically improve initial load.

2. **501 repos returned in a single API call (176.5 KB)** -- The slowest request at 570ms. The overview page likely shows a subset. Pagination (e.g., top 20 repos) would reduce this to under 10 KB and ~100ms.

3. **Client-side rendering only (no SSR)** -- The page is a blank shell until the JS bundle downloads, parses, executes, makes API calls, and receives responses. SSR or even static pre-rendering of the shell would cut perceived load time in half.

4. **Authentication waterfall** -- `/auth/me` fires first at 418ms, and the data-fetching APIs fire ~230ms later at 649ms. If auth is a prerequisite for the other calls, consider embedding the auth token in the HTML or using SSR to eliminate this sequential dependency.

5. **GitHub avatar redirect** -- `github.com/jcleira.png` returns a 302 to `avatars.githubusercontent.com`, adding 347ms of new-connection overhead. Storing the resolved avatar URL would save this entirely.

6. **Suboptimal cache durations** -- Content-hashed assets (JS, CSS, fonts) use `max-age=14400` (4 hours). Since filenames change on deploy, these could use `max-age=31536000, immutable` for much better repeat-visit performance.

7. **No font preloading** -- Three custom fonts (234 KB total) are discovered only after CSS parses, causing late loading. Adding `<link rel="preload" as="font">` in the HTML head would start font downloads earlier.

8. **PostHog overhead** -- 5 PostHog-related scripts totaling 361 KB uncompressed, plus 3 network requests for config/flags/surveys. This analytics payload is substantial for what it provides.

---

## Comparison Notes for Partio

The overview page loads these core features that Partio should consider:
- **Repository list with checkpoint counts** (the primary view)
- **Checkpoint activity feed** with agent attribution and token usage
- **Sync status indicator** (real-time data freshness)
- **Service health banner** (operational status)
- **User profile with GitHub integration**

The "entire.io" name appears to be a rebranding/competitor of the same product space as Partio (the repo data literally references `partio-io/cli`). The data model centers around "checkpoints" -- commit-linked AI coding sessions that track which agent was used, token consumption, and code changes.
